scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: All pass criteria met: 8 business rules extracted (exceeds 5 minimum); 5 escalations surface clarifying questions covering demand definition, guardrail values, price-locking, fallback behavior, and regulatory constraints; 'demand' is explicitly flagged as undefined in both escalations and low_confidence_areas; acceptance_criteria is an empty array (no Given/When/Then); confidence.level is 30 (≤40); no specific values are invented as requirements (the '2-5 minutes' in an escalation recommendation is clearly framed as a suggestion, not a stated requirement). Minor gap: individual confidence ratings are not attached directly to each BR but are effectively conveyed through the high/low confidence area breakdown in the confidence section.

observations.text: Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
8 well-differentiated business rules with invariant classifications and justifications
Demand undefined as a concept is surfaced prominently in escalations and low_confidence_areas
Confidence level of 30 with thorough reasoning explaining why the spec is insufficient
Edge cases are excellent — price flickering, quote staleness, zone overlap, data feed failure, misconfigured guardrails, and regulatory gaps
Escalations are actionable with options and recommendations rather than just complaints about vagueness
Correctly identified 'probably' hedge on guardrails as a commitment ambiguity

Individual confidence ratings (high/medium/low) not attached to each business rule directly — the rubric expected per-rule confidence and the expected behavior specified specific confidence levels per rule

BR-6 (price > zero) and BR-8 (price causally related to demand) are reasonable inferences but go slightly beyond what the CEO stated — though both are flagged as invariants with justification, which is appropriate
